methods.description: Procedure for investigating local subsurface stratigraphy and characterizing sediment properties and depositional environments in the field using coring and visual description.

steps.description: We cored several cross sections to understand the local stratigraphy (Figs. 3 and 4) using an Edelman auger and a 1-m-long gouge with 3 cm diameter.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform sediment coring using auger and gouge to create cross sections

steps.description: All the sediments were described in the field according to the US Department of Agriculture texture classification system.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe sediment texture and characteristics in the field using USDA classification
inputs: US Department of Agriculture texture classification system

steps.description: We classified the depositional environment either as chenier or as non-chenier. The cheniers were labelled as such based on their geomorphological expression, their stratigraphic position above fine-grained sediments and their sedimentological characteristics (sand and shells).
steps.searchable_summary: Classify depositional environment (chenier/non-chenier) based on characteristics

steps.description: Our deepest boreholes reach the Pleistocene substrate that is very stiff and mottled and hence easily recognizable.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the Pleistocene substrate based on stiffness and mottling

methods.description: Procedure for collecting sediment samples for Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) dating, ensuring minimal light exposure.

steps.description: The most sandy and homogenous parts of the cheniers, mostly in the center, were chosen for OSL sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify optimal sediment layers for OSL sampling based on characteristics

steps.description: For OSL sampling, we first drilled with the Edelman auger to right above the targeted level
steps.searchable_summary: Drill borehole to just above target depth using an auger

steps.description: and then attached an Eijkelkamp liner sampler, a 30-cm-long and 5-cm-wide metal cylinder with a plastic liner, to the extension rods.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble OSL sampling tool by attaching liner sampler to extension rods
inputs: Eijkelkamp liner sampler (30 cm long, 5 cm wide)

steps.description: This cylinder was then hammered into the ground.
steps.searchable_summary: Drive liner sampler into sediment at target depth

steps.description: Once lifted and detached, the liner sampler was extruded within a light-tight, black plastic bag.
steps.searchable_summary: Extrude sediment core from sampler into light-tight bag

methods.description: Procedure for collecting peat samples for radiocarbon dating using a wide gouge, targeting specific stratigraphic positions related to subdelta activity.

steps.description: The radiocarbon samples were taken with a 6-cm-wide gouge.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment/peat sample using a wide gouge

steps.description: The beginning of subdelta activity was dated by sampling the top of peat beds below the overbank deposits of the trunk channel
steps.searchable_summary: Collect radiocarbon sample from top of peat bed below overbank deposits
inputs: Identified stratigraphic layer (peat top below overbank)

steps.description: whereas the end of activity was constrained by dating the base of peat beds overlying 30 the overbank deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect radiocarbon sample from base of peat bed overlying overbank deposits
inputs: Identified stratigraphic layer (peat base above overbank)

methods.description: Methods used to determine surface elevations and geographical positions of borehole sites using existing DEM data and GPS.

steps.description: Surface elevations were obtained using DEM data (Gesch, 2007;LSU, 2011) with a vertical accuracy of about 0.25 m.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire surface elevation data from existing Digital Elevation Models (DEM)

steps.description: DEM data were also used to plot the land surface in the cross sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Plot land surface profile on stratigraphic cross sections using DEM data
outputs: Cross section diagrams with surface profile

steps.description: The geographical position of borehole sites was determined using a hand-held GPS (accuracy 5-10 m).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure geographical coordinates of borehole sites using a hand-held GPS

methods.description: Strategic approach for selecting sample types (Radiocarbon, OSL) and specific stratigraphic locations (peat tops/bases, levee tops) to constrain the timing of trunk channel and subdelta activity.

steps.description: To improve the chronology of the MDP we focused on constraining periods of activity of the trunk channels that feed the Teche, St. Bernard and Plaquemines-Modern subdeltas, but also dated some smaller distributaries that occur within these subdeltas.
steps.searchable_summary: Define research objective: date channel and subdelta activity periods

steps.description: Using the same equipment as in the CP, multiple cross sections were again constructed before sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Create stratigraphic cross sections using coring equipment before sampling

steps.description: Depending on the proximity to the main channel, they exhibit a sandy channel belt with adjacent natural-levee deposits consisting of silt loam and silty clay loam. Moving further into the flood basin, silty clay and clay become dominant and humic clay layers occur frequently. In most cases a peat bed occurs below the overbank deposits, although below the proximal natural-levee deposits peat is often eroded. Sometimes the overbank deposits are covered by a paleosol that gives way to a peat bed in the flood basin.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify key stratigraphic layers (channel belt, levee, flood basin, peat, paleosol) within cross sections

steps.description: The beginning of subdelta activity was dated by sampling the top of peat beds below the overbank deposits of the trunk channel (Radiocarbon).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect radiocarbon samples from top of peat beds below overbank deposits to date activity start

steps.description: whereas the end of activity was constrained by dating the base of peat beds overlying the overbank deposits (Radiocarbon).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect radiocarbon samples from base of peat beds overlying overbank deposits to date activity end

steps.description: As significant amounts of time can elapse before peat starts to form after channel abandonment (Törnqvist and Van Dijk, 1993), we also dated the top of natural-levee deposits using OSL to better constrain the period of activity.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect OSL samples from top of natural-levee deposits to refine activity end date
